title_en: Can Africa take the Asian path to growth?
title_zh: 非洲能否走上亚洲式的增长道路？
rubric_en: Stalling agricultural productivity makes it look unlikely
rubric_zh: 农业生产率停滞让这一前景看起来不太可能

paragraphs.en: Economists in Africa speak enviously of the “green revolution” that transformed Asia from the 1960s onwards, when new varieties of rice flourished on small family farms. Population density pushed farmers to innovate and use more inputs, like fertiliser. As rural people grew richer, they demanded more manufactured goods. Factories filled with workers who were no longer needed in the fields.
paragraphs.zh: 非洲的经济学家很羡慕自20世纪60年代起席卷亚洲的“绿色革命”，当时新的水稻品种在小型家庭农场里繁茂生长。人口密度促使农民进行创新，并使用更多投入品，比如化肥。随着农村居民变得更富裕，他们对工业品的需求也随之增加。工厂吸纳了越来越多不再需要在田间劳作的工人。

paragraphs.en: Could sub-Saharan Africa be on the same path? In aggregate its farmers are growing more cereals, such as maize (corn) and rice, than ever: nearly five times as much as in the 1960s, when many countries achieved independence. But most of those gains came from cultivating more land, which cannot go on for ever (see chart 1). Africa, once sparsely populated, is getting crowded. The amount of arable land per person has been falling for decades, and now sits at roughly the global average.
paragraphs.zh: 撒哈拉以南非洲能否走同样的路？总体而言，其农民如今种植的谷物（如玉米和大米）比以往更多：几乎是20世纪60年代的五倍左右，那时许多国家刚刚独立。但这些增产主要来自扩大种植面积，而这种做法不可能无限持续（见图表1）。曾经人口稀疏的非洲正变得拥挤。人均可耕地面积已经数十年持续下降，现在大致与全球平均水平接近。

paragraphs.en: That might not matter if farmers were also growing more crops per hectare. But recently gentle growth in agricultural productivity has given way to stagnation, perhaps even decline. Consider figures drawn from national statistics in Africa by the Food and Agriculture Organisation (FAO), a UN body. Cereal yields did not grow between 2020 and 2024, the latest data point (see chart 2). Nor did total factor productivity (TFP), a measure of how efficiently inputs of all kinds (such as labour and machinery) are turned into produce. Most African countries had lower agricultural TFP in 2023 than a decade before.
paragraphs.zh: 如果农民也在每公顷种更多作物，那么土地压力可能并不关键。然而，最近农业生产率的温和增长已经让位于停滞，甚至是下降。根据联合国粮农组织（FAO）的国家统计数据，可以看到这种趋势。到目前为止最新的数据点（见图表2）表明，谷物单产在2020年至2024年之间没有增长。总要素生产率（TFP）也没有增长；TFP衡量的是各种投入（如劳动力和机械）转化为产出的效率。2023年，大多数非洲国家的农业TFP都低于十年前。

paragraphs.en: This seems to be more than a pandemic blip. In a paper published in 2024, Douglas Gollin of Tufts University in Massachusetts and his co-authors analysed data from surveys of 55,000 household farms in six African countries between 2008 and 2019. They estimated that, for smallholdings, yields and TFP were already falling by 3-4% a year then. They found steeper declines than the FAO did, perhaps because their sample did not include large farms, or because official statistics are sketchy.
paragraphs.zh: 这似乎不仅是疫情造成的短暂波动。2024年发表的一篇论文中，美国马萨诸塞州塔夫茨大学的道格拉斯·戈林（Douglas Gollin）及其合著者分析了2008年至2019年间六个非洲国家55,000个家庭农场的调查数据。他们估计，在小规模农场中，单位产量和TFP当时就已每年下降3%至4%。他们发现的下滑幅度比FAO更陡，这可能是因为他们的样本没有包含大农场，也可能是官方统计数据本身并不完整。

paragraphs.en: Why is this happening? There are plenty of suspects, from conflict to tired soils. As El Niño returns, threatening drought in southern Africa, thoughts turn to climate change. By one estimate, agricultural TFP on the continent is a third lower than it could have been, had the climate remained as it was in 1961. Farmers complain that they no longer know when to sow.
paragraphs.zh: 为什么会这样？从冲突到土壤退化都有嫌疑。随着厄尔尼诺回归，并对南部非洲构成干旱威胁，人们的注意力转向了气候变化。按一种估算，如果非洲的气候保持在 1961 年的水平，那么该地区的农业总全要素生产率本可以高出三分之一。农民抱怨说，他们已经不知道该什么时候下种了。

paragraphs.en: Mr Gollin and his colleagues cannot rule out environmental factors, but in checking weather data they have yet to find a smoking gun. Instead, their tentative hypothesis is that workers are spending less time in their fields. Richer, better-educated farmers are especially likely to look for other work. Since factors like workers’ skills and working hours are imperfectly measured, this shows up as a fall in TFP.
paragraphs.zh: 戈林先生及其同事并未排除环境因素的影响，但在核查气象数据后，他们仍未发现明确的“烟雾弹”。相反，他们的初步假设是，务农者在田间投入的时间正在减少。更富有、更高学历的农民尤其倾向于去做其他工作。由于农民技能和劳动时长等因素难以被准确度量，这种变化会表现为农业总全要素生产率的下降。

paragraphs.en: If that proves true, the lessons from Asia would need to be revised. There, farmers responded to rising population density by working their land more intensively. But African farmers are cutting their hours most rapidly in congested places, especially near cities. That might be because African economies are relatively integrated into global markets, so city-dwellers’ growing demand for food is partly met by imports. If local food prices do not rise, farmers have less reason to intensify.
paragraphs.zh: 如果这个判断成立，那么亚洲的经验教训就需要修正。在亚洲，农民面对人口密度上升，通常是通过更集约地耕作来应对。但在非洲，农民在拥挤地区——尤其是城市周边——减少劳动时间的速度最快。这可能是因为非洲经济与全球市场联系得更紧密，城市居民对粮食的需求部分由进口来弥补。如果本地粮价不上涨，农民就缺乏增加耕作强度的动力。

paragraphs.en: Another difference is that in Asia it was rising crop yields that helped kick-start economic change. In Africa agricultural productivity is stagnant, but farmers are still being pulled away from their land. That might be because money from sources such as oil revenues is driving urban growth and creating informal service jobs, suggests Mr Gollin.
paragraphs.zh: 另一处差异在于，亚洲的经济转型是由作物产量提高起步推动的。而在非洲，农业生产率停滞不前，但农民却仍然被从土地上吸走。戈林先生据此推测，这可能是石油收益等收入来源正在推动城市增长，并创造了非正式服务业岗位。

paragraphs.en: The thorniest question concerns farm size. In Asia, small farms yielded more per hectare than large ones. Some studies find the same relationship in Africa; others explain it away as measurement error. In a paper in 2019 on Kenya, Milu Muyanga and Thomas Jayne, both of Michigan State University, find a U-shaped pattern: yields fall as farms get bigger, but in farms larger than five hectares they start to rise again, perhaps because it becomes easier to mechanise. Often even highly productive small farms are too tiny to be viable.
paragraphs.zh: 最棘手的问题在于农场规模。在亚洲，小农场每公顷产量高于大农场。一些研究也发现了非洲的同样规律；也有研究则认为这只是测量误差所致。在2019年关于肯尼亚的一篇论文中，米卢·穆扬加和托马斯·杰恩（都来自密歇根州立大学）发现了一个U形关系：农场越大，产量先下降，但当农场规模超过五公顷后又开始上升，也许是因为此时更容易实现机械化。即便是高效的小农场，也常常太小，难以持续经营。

paragraphs.en: Admirers of the Asian model point to rising fertiliser use in the 2010s, suggesting that some farms are starting to intensify. Fast-growing trading firms are shuttling farm produce to towns; demand for meat, eggs and vegetables is met almost entirely by domestic supply. Since 2005 the market value of Africa’s agricultural produce has risen at a rate that matches South Asia, and outstrips every other region of the world. Crowded spots, from Africa’s Great Lakes to the Ethiopian highlands, could yet follow the path of Taiwan or Punjab.
paragraphs.zh: 亚洲模式的拥护者指出，2010年代肥料使用量上升，说明一些农场正在走向更高强度的经营。快速增长的贸易公司正在把农产品运往城镇；对肉类、鸡蛋和蔬菜的需求几乎完全由国内供应满足。自2005年以来，非洲农产品的市场价值增长速度与南亚持平，并且超过了世界其他所有地区。非洲“大裂谷”到埃塞俄比亚高原等人口密集区域，仍有可能走上台湾或旁遮普的道路。

paragraphs.en: Still, the relevance of the Asian example must be proved rather than assumed. “We’re in a different world than we were in [Asia] in the 1960s,” Mr Gollin says. Africa must find its own way. ■
paragraphs.zh: 尽管如此，亚洲的例子不能被当作理所当然的模板。"我们处在一个与上世纪六十年代亚洲完全不同的世界，"戈林先生说。非洲必须找到自己的道路。■

paragraphs.en: Sign up to the Analysing Africa, a weekly newsletter that keeps you in the loop about the world’s youngest—and least understood—continent.
paragraphs.zh: 订阅《非洲观察》（Analysing Africa），这份每周通讯能让你及时了解世界上最年轻、也最不为人所熟知的这个大陆。
